station_address: Western Express Hwy, near Domestic Airport, Navpada, Vile Parle East, Vile Parle, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400099
text:
Located strategically near the Vile Parle airport area, this Indian Oil outlet is a frequent stop for many travelers and locals alike. While there are several things they get right, there are a few persistent operational issues that prevent it from being a top-tier station.

The Positives: Hospitality and Maintenance
The most impressive aspect of this pump is the conduct of the staff. In a city where fuel station employees are often rushed and dismissive, the team here stands out. They are polite, professional, and well-coordinated in managing the constant flow of vehicles, especially during peak airport hours.
Another huge benefit is the Free Nitrogen Air facility.
Most stations in Mumbai either have broken air machines or charge extra for Nitrogen.
Here, the machine is usually functional, and the service is free, which is a massive plus for vehicle maintenance and tire longevity.
The fuel quality also seems consistent, and I’ve never had an issue with the "zero-check" or the accuracy of the dispensing units.

The Major Drawback: Payment & Connectivity
The reason for my 3-star rating boils down to the frequent and frustrating payment failures. In a digital-first economy, being told "the server is down" is a significant inconvenience.

Digital Payment Reliability: On several visits, they have refused to accept Credit/Debit cards or UPI (Google Pay/PhonePe), citing server issues or machine connectivity problems.

The Cash Scramble: Since this pump is near the airport, many customers are on their way to a flight or have just landed. Not everyone carries enough cash for a full tank of fuel. Being told at the last minute that online payments aren't working creates unnecessary stress and delays.

Areas for Improvement
While the staff is doing their best, the management needs to prioritize their IT infrastructure.

Backup Systems: They should invest in a more stable internet connection or have multiple POS (Point of Sale) machines from different banks to ensure card payments always go through.

Advance Warning: If the server is truly down, there should be a prominent sign at the entrance so customers aren't caught off guard after the fuel has already been pumped.

Final Verdict
Go here for the polite service and the free Nitrogen, but always carry cash just in case. It’s a reliable station for fuel quality, but the recurring "server down" excuse for digital payments is a hurdle that needs to be fixed to earn back those extra stars.
